content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Amar Shergill 
1104 Corporate Way, Suite 101 
Sacramento, CA 95831-3875 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-19-140 
Dear Mr. Shergill: 
August 16, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act ("Act"). 1 Because your inquiry is general in nature, we are treating your 
request as one for informal assistance. 2 
Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not 
the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
If the Progressive Caucus of the California Democratic Party establishes a political action 
committee ("PAC") intended to conduct activity related only to local non-partisan races, will the 
PAC be considered independent from the California Democratic Party ("CDP") or will 
contributions to the PAC be considered contributions to the CDP? 
CONCLUSION 
Contributions to the PAC are not required to be aggregated with contributions to the CDP if 
the PAC will be conducting activity related only to local non-partisan races and there is no 
involvement of the CDP in the PAC's operations. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 


FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
File No. I-19-140 
Page No. 2 
The Progressive Caucus of the CDP intends to establish a PAC for local non-partisan races. 
The CDP does not generally fundraise or contribute to local non-partisan races. The Progressive 
Caucus was established under the bylaws of the CDP, yet the Caucus has an independent board 
from the CDP. The CDP will have no involvement in the PAC or its operations. The PAC will raise 
funds through private fundraising, not from CDP events or accounts. There will be no joint 
agreement between the CDP and the PAC for raising funds and the PAC will not be operating as an 
intermediary for the CDP. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 82013 provides that a "committee" means any person or combination of persons 
who directly or indirectly does any of the following: 
(a) Receives contributions totaling two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more in a calendar 
year; 
(b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a 
calendar year; or 
( c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars ($10,000) or more in a calendar year 
to or at the behest of candidates or committees. 
YotJ have indicated that the PAC will be conducting private fundraising. Therefore, if the 
PAC raises contributions of $2,000 or more it will qualify as a committee under the Act. 
Section 85303 provides limits on contributions to committees that conduct activity involving 
candidates for state elective office. You have stated that the PAC will not be supporting or opposing 
state candidates or their committees. The PAC will solely operate on the local level and there will 
be no involvement of the CDP with the PAC. As such, there is no concern that state contribution 
limits will be avoided. Therefore, the PAC may operate independently from the CDP and 
contributions to the PAC need not be aggregated with contributions to the CDP. However, we 
advise the PAC to contact the localjurisdiction(s) in which it will operate to ascertain whether local 
contribution limits or other restrictions apply. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
RPOC:aja 
Sincerely, 
· Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
~ 
e>~/cr 
Ryan O'Connor 
'counsel, Legal Division

CONCLUSION: Contributions to the PAC are not required to be aggregated with contributions to the CDP if
